title: May begins in UP with cool winds, rain: Temperatures drop, heatwave relief likely for 10 days - Bhaskar English
full_text:
May begins in UP with cool winds, rain:Temperatures drop, heatwave relief likely for 10 days
The start of May in Uttar Pradesh has brought relief, with light rain and strong winds over the past three days leading to a drop in temperatures on Friday. Cool winds throughout the day provided respite from intense heat, and the weather remained pleasant.
Maximum temperatures in several districts remained well below normal. Gorakhpur recorded a maximum of 26.4°C — the third lowest in observation history — while Ballia logged 27.5°C, the fifth lowest on record.
In 15 districts, maximum temperatures ranged between 30°C and 32°C. In contrast, Orai was the hottest at 41.6°C, while Hardoi recorded a minimum temperature of 17°C.
See weather pictures-
Good rainfall in 24 hours; fresh rain expected from May 4
Several districts recorded substantial rainfall in the past 24 hours — Kannauj (54 mm), Balrampur (52.6 mm), Lakhimpur Kheri (51.8 mm), Bareilly (29 mm), and Gorakhpur (23.8 mm). The rain helped lower temperatures and provided relief from heat.
According to Dr Atul Singh, senior scientist at the Regional Meteorological Centre in Lucknow, the weather is expected to remain dry for the next two days, with temperatures likely to rise by 6 to 8°C due to sunshine. A fresh western disturbance is expected to become active from May 4, which may trigger another spell of storms and rain. No heatwave expected in early May
As per the monthly forecast, Uttar Pradesh is likely to receive above-normal rainfall in May 2026. However, heatwave days may be above normal in the Terai region, while eastern UP is expected to see normal conditions. Overall, maximum and minimum temperatures across most parts of the state are likely to remain normal or slightly above normal.
The weather department has clarified that no heatwave conditions are expected during the first 10 days of May due to active weather systems.
Rain damages crops; CM orders compensation survey
Continuous rainfall has caused damage to crops, particularly vegetables. Farmers fear losses to cucumber, melon, and watermelon crops, with concerns of rotting due to excess moisture.
Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath has directed officials to assess the damage on the ground and prepare reports swiftly so that compensation can be provided to affected farmers.
How will the weather be for the next 5 days?
- May 2: New western disturbance becomes active; clouds likely over eastern UP with possible rain and strong winds.
- May 3: Light rain with thunder possible in parts of eastern and western UP; low storm activity.
- May 4: Statewide rain and storms expected; humidity likely due to intermittent sunshine.
- May 5: Continued storm and rain activity; chances of lightning and hail in some areas.
- May 6: Cloudy skies across the state; light rain and strong winds likely in some regions.
Mixed conditions ahead: warm nights, localised heat
Some areas may still experience heatwave conditions during afternoons, along with warmer nights. A fresh spell of rain and thunderstorms may bring a temperature drop of 2–4°C, improving heatwave conditions. Rain activity is expected to begin in western UP and gradually move eastward.
El Niño concern for monsoon
Between June and September, Uttar Pradesh and the rest of the country may receive below-normal rainfall. IMD Director General Dr M Mohapatra said conditions are shifting from La Niña to El Niño in the Pacific Ocean, which typically leads to reduced rainfall. Lower snow cover in the northern hemisphere between January and March may also impact the monsoon.
Know about El-Nio and La-Nia-
- El Niño: Sea surface temperatures rise by 3–4°C; occurs roughly twice in 10 years; leads to reduced rainfall in typically wetter regions and increased rainfall in drier areas.La Niña: Rapid cooling of ocean waters; leads to increased cloud formation and generally good rainfall globally.
